growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: measured over time (Fig 1) but no numeric value or specific rate given in text; glucose remained high throughout, culture not carbon-limited

sec.units: µg/mL at 63 h (volumetric titre, not a specific rate)

growth_records.cite: Del Carratore et al. Multi-omics Study of Planobispora rosea, Producer of the Thiopeptide Antibiotic GE2270A. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00341-21. [PMC8269224]
growth_records.snippet: P. rosea ATCC 53733 was grown in medium C... After 24 h, the culture entered stationary phase, following which GE2270A was found to accumulate to about 50 μg/ml at 63 h. Throughout the fermentation, the amount of free glucose... remained hi
growth_records.notes: Batch fermentation; biomass, glucose and GE2270A were measured over time but numeric values are only in Fig 1 (not in text), so no growth rate or specific uptake rate could be computed. Culture entered stationary phase after 24 h; not carbon-limited. NOTE: the paper's genome-scale model uses glucose Vmax=10 mmol/gDW/h, O2=15 mmol/gDW/h and glucose Km~3 mmol/L, but these are literature-derived E. coli (Varma & Palsson) constraints for FBA simulation, NOT measured P. rosea rates, so they are deliberately not recorded as flux data. Supplementation variants (medium C + 1 or 5 mM phosphate, or + 0.25 or 1.2 mM FeCl3; 100 mL, 30°C, monitored every 24 h up to 7 d) were also run but likewise report no numeric rates in text.

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 16575 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/16575

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
